en-US.name: Concrete Cure: Form Strength
en-US.subtitle: Set & Strip Timeline by PSI
en-US.promotional_text: Just poured concrete? Track local temperature over time and know when it is strong enough to strip forms, walk on, and drive on - not the calendar.
en-US.description:
When can you strip the forms, walk on it, or drive on it? Concrete Cure answers that from temperature over time - the maturity method - instead of a generic 28-day rule that ignores how cold or warm it actually is.

Concrete does not gain strength by the calendar. The same slab can take three to four times longer at 40F than at 70F, and below freezing the clock effectively pauses. A surface can look and feel hard while the inside is still far too weak to load. Strip or load too early and the pour can crack, sag, or fail.

HOW IT WORKS

1. Enter your pour: design PSI, cement type, water-cement ratio, thickness, and element (slab, wall, column, or footing).
2. Concrete Cure pulls live hourly temperature for your location and accumulates maturity hour by hour.
3. You get today's estimated strength and the time each milestone is reached - strip forms, walk, drive, and full design strength.

Under a minute to set up. No account required.

CORE FEATURES

Today's Strength
Your pour's estimated strength right now as a percentage and PSI, with the reason - including whether cold weather has stalled the gain. Built on the Nurse-Saul maturity method with cement type, water-cement ratio, thickness, and element factored in.

Next Milestone
When your pour reaches the next threshold, based on your actual local temperature so far and the forecast ahead.

Manual Temperature
No signal at the job site? Enter the temperature yourself and keep the estimate going.

PRO FEATURES
Upgrade to Pro for deeper insights:
- Full milestone timeline: strip, walk, drive, and full strength
- Track multiple pours at once
- Push notifications when each milestone is reached
- Cold-weather and freeze-stall alerts
- Plastic-shrinkage and evaporation-rate warnings
- Pour history and field-test calibration

PRIVACY FIRST
- No account, no login
- All data stored locally on your device
- Location is used solely to fetch local temperature data, never stored on any server
- No tracking, no ads, no third-party analytics

Concrete Cure provides general strength estimates based on the Nurse-Saul maturity method and public temperature data. It is not a substitute for guidance from a qualified engineer or physical strength testing such as cylinder breaks.
